docs.title: Predictive Model for Anticipating Structural Governance Failures
docs.text: This model forecasts the likelihood of systemic governance risk across the corpus by combining drift clustering, SLA pressure, automation stability, and metadata quality signals.

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text: To predict systemic governance failures before they occur, enabling proactive intervention.

docs.title: A. Drift Cluster Density (DCD)
docs.text: Number and size of drift clusters detected in the last 30 days.

docs.title: B. Cross-Appendix Correlation (CAC)
docs.text: Percentage of drift patterns shared across 2 or more appendices.

docs.title: C. SLA Stress Index (SSI)
docs.text: Weighted measure of sla-at-risk and sla-breached items.

docs.title: D. Automation Stability Score (ASS)
docs.text: CI accuracy, webhook uptime, workflow success rate.

docs.title: E. Schema Divergence Ratio (SDR)
docs.text: Percentage of documents not aligned with current schema version.

docs.title: F. Metadata Quality Score (MQS)
docs.text: Average metadata quality across affected appendices.

docs.title: 3. Normalization
docs.text: DCD_n = min(1, DCD / 10) CAC_n = CAC SSI_n = SSI ASS_n = 1 - ASS SDR_n = SDR MQS_n = 1 - (MQS / 100)

docs.title: 4. Weighted Formula
docs.text: SystemicRisk = 100 * ( 0.25 * DCD_n + 0.20 * CAC_n + 0.20 * SSI_n + 0.15 * ASS_n + 0.10 * SDR_n + 0.10 * MQS_n )

docs.title: 5. Interpretation
docs.text: Score Meaning 0-29 Low systemic risk 30-59 Moderate systemic risk 60-79 High systemic risk 80-100 Critical systemic risk

docs.title: Moderate (30-59)
docs.text:
• Increase monitoring
  • Review schema alignment

docs.title: High (60-79)
docs.text:
• Governance steward review
  • CI + workflow evaluation

docs.title: Critical (80-100)
docs.text:
• Immediate governance intervention
  • Systemic drift RCA
  • Schema/CI/workflow updates

docs.title: Visual Architecture of How Governance Failures Spread Across the Runtime
docs.text: This diagram visualizes the Systemic-Risk Propagation Engine (SRPE), showing how drift, SLA stress, schema divergence, and automation instability propagate into systemic governance failures.

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text: To provide a visual reference for understanding how governance failures originating at the local level cascade through structural, referential, schema, and SLA coupling channels into systemic failures.

docs.title: 2. Mermaid Diagram
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    A[Local Drift] --> B[Cluster Drift]
    B --> C[Cross-Appendix Drift]
    C --> D[Automation-Driven Drift]
    D --> E[Systemic Governance Failure]

    A -. Structural Coupling .-> B
    B -. Referential Coupling .-> C
    C -. Schema Divergence .-> D
    D -. SLA Cascade .-> E

docs.title: 3. Propagation Stages
docs.text:
• Local Drift: Isolated field-level or structural violations in a single document
  • Cluster Drift: Drift spreading to related documents through structural coupling
  • Cross-Appendix Drift: Propagation across ownership boundaries via referential coupling
  • Automation-Driven Drift: Schema divergence triggering automated enforcement failures
  • Systemic Governance Failure: Full corpus integrity breakdown with SLA cascade

docs.title: 4. Coupling Types
docs.text: Coupling Type Description Structural Coupling Shared schema fields that propagate schema violations Referential Coupling Cross-document ID references that propagate integrity failures Schema Divergence Schema version fragmentation accelerating drift spread SLA Cascade SLA breaches triggering escalation across owners

docs.title: 5. Governance Controls
docs.text:
• Schema version pinning to prevent divergence
  • CI validator hardening to catch propagation early
  • Workflow resilience to maintain automation under chaos
  • Owner reassignment when reliability trend declines
  • Systemic drift intervention at Cluster Drift stage

docs.title: Automated Modeling of How Governance Failures Spread Across the Corpus
docs.text: The Systemic-Risk Propagation Engine (SRPE) predicts how local drift, SLA stress, schema divergence, and automation instability propagate into systemic governance failures.

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text: To forecast: drift spread across appendices, SLA cascade failures, schema divergence acceleration, automation-driven systemic drift, and cross-owner reliability collapse.

docs.title: A. Drift Propagation Factors
docs.text:
• Structural coupling
  • Referential coupling
  • Ownership coupling
  • Automation coupling

docs.title: B. SLA Stress Factors
docs.text:
• SLA-at-risk density
  • SLA breach velocity
  • Owner SLA trend slope

docs.title: C. Schema Factors
docs.text:
• Schema divergence
  • Deprecated field reappearance
  • Schema version fragmentation

docs.title: D. Automation Factors
docs.text:
• CI validator instability
  • Webhook event loss
  • Workflow failure probability

docs.title: Step 1 - Compute Local Drift Pressure
docs.text: LDP = DV_n + DSI_n + SDR_n

docs.title: Step 2 - Compute Coupling Pressure
docs.text: CP = SC + RC + OC + AC

docs.title: Step 3 - Compute SLA Cascade Pressure
docs.text: SCP = SLA_risk + SLA_trend + Owner_decline

docs.title: Step 4 - Compute Automation Instability
docs.text: AI = 1 - AS

docs.title: 4. Outputs
docs.text:
• Propagation score (0-100)
  • Drift propagation map
  • SLA cascade forecast
  • Schema divergence forecast
  • Automation instability forecast
  • Recommended governance intervention

docs.title: 5. Governance Actions
docs.text:
• Update schema
  • Patch CI validator
  • Patch workflows
  • Reassign owners
  • Trigger systemic drift review

docs.title: Visualizing How Governance Risk Spreads Across Owners, Appendices, and Drift Types
docs.text: This heatmap models how systemic governance risk propagates across the corpus based on drift clustering, SLA stress, schema divergence, and automation instability.

docs.title: X-Axis - Appendices
docs.text: Sorted by: (1) Drift density, (2) Schema divergence, (3) Owner reliability

docs.title: Y-Axis - Propagation Factors
docs.text:
• Structural coupling
  • Referential coupling
  • Ownership coupling
  • Automation coupling
  • Schema divergence
  • SLA stress

docs.title: 2. Cell Value Formula
docs.text:
PropagationRisk = FactorWeight * NormalizedValue

Where: - Structural coupling = 4 - Referential coupling = 3 - Ownership coupling = 2 - Automation coupling = 3 - Schema divergence = 4 - SLA stress = 5

docs.title: 3. Color Scale
docs.text:
Score Color 0-4 #E8F5E9 (light green) 5-9 #C8E6C9 (green) 10-14 #FFF9C4 (yellow) 15-20 #FFE082 (orange) >20 #FF8A80 (red)

Override: Any appendix with >= 2 systemic drift clusters -> #D32F2F (deep red).

docs.title: 4. Data Sources
docs.text:
• Drift clustering engine
  • drift_events
  • sla_timers
  • owner_reliability_snapshots
  • Schema version map
  • Automation stability metrics

docs.title: 5. Update Frequency
docs.text:
• Propagation factors: hourly
  • SLA stress: real-time
  • Schema divergence: daily

docs.title: 6. Interactions
docs.text:
• Hover: show propagation factors
  • Click: open appendix detail panel
  • Filters: drift type, owner, time range

docs.title: Simulating How Local Failures Escalate into Systemic Governance Risk
docs.text: This simulator models how local drift, SLA stress, schema divergence, and automation instability propagate into systemic governance failures.

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text:
To test:

  • How quickly local drift becomes systemic
  • Which couplings (structural, referential, ownership, automation) drive propagation
  • How SLA cascades and schema divergence amplify risk
  • Which interventions most effectively contain propagation

docs.title: A. Corpus and Topology
docs.text:
• Document graph (structural and referential links)
  • Owner graph (ownership coupling)
  • Automation graph (CI, workflows, webhooks)
  • Schema version map

docs.title: B. Initial Conditions
docs.text:
• Initial drift set (documents, severities)
  • Initial SLA state (at-risk, breached)
  • Initial automation stability (CI, webhook, workflow)
  • Initial schema divergence level

docs.title: C. Propagation Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Symbol Description Structural coupling weight SC How strongly structural links transmit drift Referential coupling weight RC How strongly referential links transmit drift Ownership coupling weight OC How strongly shared ownership transmits drift Automation coupling weight AC How strongly automation failures transmit drift SLA cascade sensitivity SCS Amplification of SLA stress on propagation Schema divergence sensitivity SDS Amplification of schema drift on propagation

docs.title: 3. Simulation Loop
docs.text: At each timestep t:

docs.title: Step 1: Compute Local Drift Pressure
docs.text:
LDP_i(t) = f(Drift_i, Severity_i, SDR_i)

Where SDR_i is the schema divergence rate for document i.

docs.title: Step 2: Compute Coupling Pressure
docs.text: CP_i(t) = SC_i + RC_i + OC_i + AC_i

docs.title: Step 3: Compute SLA Cascade Pressure
docs.text: SCP_i(t) = SCS * SLA_risk_i(t)

docs.title: Step 4: Compute Automation Instability
docs.text:
AI_i(t) = 1 - AS_i(t)

Where AS_i(t) is automation stability at timestep t.

docs.title: Step 5: Propagation Probability
docs.text: P_prop_i(t) = sigmoid(alpha*LDP_i + beta*CP_i + gamma*SCP_i + delta*AI_i)

docs.title: Step 6: State Update
docs.text:
• Sample propagation events based on P_prop_i(t)
  • Update drift set, SLA state, schema divergence, and automation stability
  • Record propagation extent for time-series output

docs.title: 4. Outputs
docs.text:
• Time-series of propagation extent (documents, appendices, owners)
  • Systemic-risk score over time
  • SLA cascade curves
  • Schema divergence curves
  • Automation instability curves
  • Intervention effectiveness comparison across playbook strategies

docs.title: 5. Use Cases
docs.text:
• Stress-testing governance runtime against worst-case drift scenarios
  • Validating containment playbooks before deployment
  • Tuning systemic-risk detection thresholds
  • Prioritizing schema hardening and automation resilience investments

docs.title: 6. Intervention Scenarios
docs.text: Scenario Intervention Expected Outcome Baseline None Unconstrained propagation Schema pin Pin schema version at t=5 Reduce schema divergence spread Owner reassignment Reassign at-risk owners at t=10 Break ownership coupling chain CI hardening Increase automation stability to 0.99 Reduce automation instability pressure Full containment All interventions at t=5 Rapid propagation suppression

docs.title: ADR Metadata Contract
docs.text: This contract defines the canonical frontmatter schema for all Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) in the UIAO corpus.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text:
---
id: ADR-012                # Unique ADR identifier (ADR-<number>)
title: Federated Drift Detection
status: Accepted           # One of: Proposed, Accepted, Superseded, Deprecated, Replaced
date: 2026-04-07           # ISO date of decision
deciders:                  # People or roles who made the decision
  - architecture-board@example.com
consulted:                 # Optional: stakeholders consulted
  - data-governance@example.com
tags:                      # Cross-fabric tags
  - drift
  - evidence
  - governance
affects:                   # UIAO documents impacted by this ADR
  - UIAO_C_01
  - UIAO_D_02
supersedes: ADR-005        # Optional: ADR this one supersedes
supersededBy: ADR-020      # Optional: ADR that supersedes this one
uiao:
  id: UIAO_ADR_012         # UIAO id for the ADR document itself
  status: Current
  owner: Governance Board
  lastUpdated: 2026-04-07
  commit: <short-hash>
---

docs.title: Field Semantics
docs.text:
id -- Stable ADR identifier (ADR-<number>).

title -- Short, descriptive decision title.

status -- One of:

Value Meaning Proposed Under review, not yet binding Accepted Active and authoritative Superseded Replaced by another ADR Deprecated Being phased out Replaced Explicitly replaced by another ADR

date -- Date the decision was made (or proposed). ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD).

deciders -- Roles or groups accountable for the decision.

consulted -- Optional list of stakeholders consulted prior to decision.

tags -- Used for cross-fabric queries. Canonical values: identity, drift, evidence, adapter, governance.

affects -- List of UIAO_<AppendixLetter>_<Number> document IDs impacted by this ADR.

supersedes / supersededBy -- Maintain decision lineage across the ADR lifecycle.

uiao block -- Aligns ADRs with the general UIAO document metadata contract:

Sub-field Meaning uiao.id UIAO document ID for the ADR itself (UIAO_ADR_NNN) uiao.status Document lifecycle status (Current, Draft, etc.) uiao.owner Accountable team or role uiao.lastUpdated Last modification date uiao.commit Short Git commit hash at time of last update

docs.title: Validation Rules
docs.text:
All ADRs must conform to this schema. CI enforces presence and allowed values for:

  • id -- required, pattern ADR-[0-9]+
  • title -- required, non-empty string
  • status -- required, one of the five allowed values above
  • date -- required, ISO 8601 date
  • uiao.id -- required, pattern UIAO_ADR_[0-9]+

Fields deciders, consulted, tags, affects, supersedes, and supersededBy are optional but strongly recommended for traceability.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• ADR Process and Lifecycle
  • ADR Index
  • Corpus Status Dashboard

docs.title: UIAO Governance Metadata Playbook
docs.text: A practical, operator-ready guide explaining how UIAO metadata works, why it exists, and how contributors must use it to maintain a drift-free, provenance-correct documentation corpus.

docs.title: 1. Purpose of This Playbook
docs.text:
UIAO is a governance-first system. Metadata is not decoration -- it is the control plane for: provenance, drift detection, cross-fabric relationships, document lifecycle, dashboard population, ADR impact analysis, contributor workflows, and auditability.

This playbook explains: what metadata is required, why it exists, how it is validated, how it flows through the system, and how contributors must work with it.

This is the human-readable companion to:

  • UIAO Document Metadata Contract
  • UIAO ADR Metadata Contract
  • ADR Metadata Reviewer Checklist

docs.title: Layer 1 -- Human-Readable Metadata (Frontmatter)
docs.text: This is what contributors edit: title, summary, uiao.id, uiao.status, uiao.owner, uiao.tags, uiao.created, uiao.lastUpdated, uiao.commit, and ADR-only fields. This layer is authoritative for humans.

docs.title: Layer 2 -- Machine-Readable Metadata (JSON Schema)
docs.text: This is what CI validates: required fields, allowed enum values, ID patterns, date formats, non-empty tags, and ADR references to valid UIAO documents. This layer is authoritative for machines.

docs.title: Layer 3 -- Derived Metadata (Dashboards & Indexes)
docs.text: Generated automatically: corpus-index.json, dashboard tables, cross-fabric impact maps, and governance footer provenance. This layer is authoritative for reporting.

docs.title: Responsibility 1 -- Every Document Must Have a Canonical ID
docs.text:
Pattern: UIAO_<AppendixLetter>_<Number>

Example Scope UIAO_A_01 Appendix A document UIAO_E_03 Governance Plane document UIAO_META_DOC_01 Meta document UIAO_ADR_012 ADR document

IDs are immutable once assigned.

docs.title: Responsibility 2 -- Every Document Must Declare Its Status
docs.text:
Allowed values: Current, Draft, Needs Replacing, Needs Creating, Deprecated.

Status drives: dashboard alerts, governance banner health, review prioritization, and cross-fabric risk analysis.

docs.title: Responsibility 3 -- Every Document Must Declare Its Owner
docs.text: Owners are accountable for correctness, updates, cross-fabric alignment, and responding to ADR impacts. Owners must be roles or teams, not individuals.

docs.title: Responsibility 4 -- Every Document Must Declare Its Tags
docs.text:
Tags enable cross-fabric queries, dashboard filtering, ADR impact analysis, and governance plane reporting.

Allowed tags: identity, drift, evidence, adapter, governance, meta, architecture, operations, compliance.

docs.title: Responsibility 5 -- Every Document Must Declare Provenance
docs.text: Provenance includes uiao.created, uiao.lastUpdated, and uiao.commit. The commit hash is updated automatically by CI on merge.

docs.title: 4. Metadata Flow Through the System
docs.text: Step Actor Action 1 Contributor Writes frontmatter (title, summary, uiao block) 2 CI Validates required fields, allowed values, ID patterns 3 CI (on merge) Updates corpus-index.json, dashboard, provenance footer 4 MkDocs build Renders dashboard tables, ADR index, governance footer 5 Governance Metadata powers drift detection, cross-fabric analysis, audit trails

docs.title: 5. Contributor Workflow (Metadata-Focused)
docs.text:
Before editing: Check dashboard for document status. Check ADR index for impacts. Confirm correct uiao.id and ownership.

While editing: Update summary and uiao.lastUpdated. Do not change uiao.created or uiao.id. Add or update tags. Ensure status badge is correct.

Before opening PR: Ensure frontmatter matches contract. Ensure ADRs reference valid UIAO ids.

During review: Reviewers check metadata correctness, cross-fabric impacts, ADR implications, ownership correctness, and status accuracy.

After merge: CI updates commit hash, dashboard, and provenance footer. Contributor verifies dashboard entry and notifies owners if cross-fabric impact exists.

docs.title: 6. Metadata Anti-Patterns (Prohibited)
docs.text:
These are merge blockers:

  • Missing uiao.id
  • Duplicate IDs
  • Missing tags or owner
  • Missing summary
  • Incorrect status or date formats
  • Missing provenance
  • ADRs without affects
  • Documents without status badges
  • Documents referencing deprecated IDs

docs.title: 7. Governance Rules for Metadata Evolution
docs.text: Metadata contracts may only be changed via ADR (structural changes) or Governance Plane decision (operational changes). All changes must be backward compatible, include migration scripts, CI updates, and dashboard updates.

docs.title: 8. Metadata Quick Reference
docs.text:
Required for all documents: title, summary, uiao.id, uiao.status, uiao.owner, uiao.tags, uiao.created, uiao.lastUpdated, uiao.commit.

Required for ADRs additionally: id, status, date, deciders, tags, affects, summary, uiao block.

docs.title: 9. Closing Principle
docs.text: Metadata is governance. It is the mechanism by which UIAO maintains correctness, provenance, sovereignty, auditability, drift resistance, and cross-fabric integrity.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• UIAO Document Metadata Contract
  • UIAO ADR Metadata Contract
  • Governance Metadata Reviewer Checklist
  • Metadata Drift Detection Report

docs.title: Governance Metadata Reviewer Checklist
docs.text: Reviewers must confirm all items below before approving a PR.

docs.title: 1. Identity & Provenance
docs.text:
• uiao.id exists and matches UIAO_<AppendixLetter>_<Number>
  • ID is immutable (unchanged from previous version)
  • uiao.created is unchanged
  • uiao.lastUpdated is updated for this PR
  • uiao.commit placeholder present (CI will populate)
  • Status badge present in top 8 lines

docs.title: 2. Status & Ownership
docs.text:
• uiao.status is one of: Current / Draft / Needs Replacing / Needs Creating / Deprecated
  • Status accurately reflects document maturity
  • uiao.owner is a team or role, not an individual
  • Ownership is correct for the appendix or governance area

docs.title: 3. Tags & Fabric Alignment
docs.text:
• uiao.tags present and non-empty
  • Tags correctly reflect cross-fabric domain
  • fabric field (if present) matches primary domain

docs.title: 4. Summary & Title
docs.text:
• summary exists and is 1-3 sentences
  • title matches the H1 heading
  • Document purpose is clear and aligned with canon

docs.title: 5. ADR-Specific (if ADR)
docs.text:
• id matches ADR-<number>
  • status is valid (Proposed / Accepted / Superseded / Deprecated / Replaced)
  • date is ISO-correct
  • deciders present
  • affects contains valid UIAO ids
  • supersedes / supersededBy correct if present

docs.title: 6. Cross-Fabric Integrity
docs.text:
• Document does not contradict other fabrics
  • Cross-references updated if needed
  • ADR impacts reflected in affected documents
  • No references to deprecated or superseded documents

docs.title: 7. Governance & Drift Resistance
docs.text:
• No references to previous versions
  • No duplicated content across appendices
  • No ambiguous ownership
  • No missing metadata fields
  • Document follows canonical structure

docs.title: 8. Final Checks
docs.text:
• CI metadata validator passes
  • Links validated
  • Diagrams include alt text
  • Document renders correctly in site preview

If any item fails, the PR must not be approved.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Governance Metadata Playbook
  • UIAO Document Metadata Contract
  • UIAO ADR Metadata Contract

docs.title: Metadata Drift Detection Report
docs.text: Copy this template for each drift detection run. Replace all <placeholders>.

docs.title: 1. Report Metadata
docs.text:
• Report ID: DRIFT-<number>
  • Generated On: <date>
  • Generated By: <system or reviewer>
  • Scope: Full corpus / Appendix X / ADRs / Specific documents

docs.title: 2. Summary of Findings
docs.text:
• Total Documents Scanned: <number>
  • Documents With Drift: <number>
  • Severity: Low / Medium / High / Critical
  • Primary Causes:
    • Missing metadata
    • Invalid values
    • Outdated provenance
    • Cross-fabric inconsistency
    • ADR mismatch

docs.title: 3.1 Missing Metadata
docs.text: Document ID Missing Fields Notes UIAO_A_01 uiao.owner Owner not declared

docs.title: 3.2 Invalid Metadata
docs.text: Document ID Field Value Expected UIAO_C_03 status Active Current

docs.title: 3.3 Provenance Drift
docs.text: Document ID lastUpdated commit Notes UIAO_E_02 2025-01-01 <old> Not updated in 12 months

docs.title: 3.4 Cross-Fabric Drift
docs.text: Document ID Conflict Impact UIAO_D_04 Evidence contradicts Drift Fabric Requires ADR

docs.title: 4. Required Actions
docs.text:
• Update metadata fields
  • Correct invalid values
  • Refresh provenance
  • File ADR for cross-fabric conflict
  • Notify document owners

docs.title: 5. Owner Notifications
docs.text: Owner Documents Action Required identity-governance@example.com UIAO_B_02 Update tags

docs.title: 6. Resolution Summary
docs.text:
• Resolved: <number>
  • Pending: <number>
  • Escalated: <number>

docs.title: 7. Sign-Off
docs.text:
• Reviewed By: <name or role>
  • Approved By: <governance board>

docs.text:
• Governance Metadata Playbook
  • Governance Metadata Reviewer Checklist
  • Corpus Status Dashboard

docs.title: UIAO ADR Metadata Contract
docs.text: This contract defines the canonical frontmatter schema for all UIAO ADRs. It is parallel to the UIAO Document Metadata Contract and must be enforced by CI.

docs.title: Required Frontmatter Schema
docs.text:
---
title: Federated Drift Detection

id: ADR-012                    # ADR identifier (ADR-<number>)
status: Accepted               # Proposed | Accepted | Superseded | Deprecated | Replaced
date: 2026-04-07               # Decision date (ISO)

deciders:                      # Required: accountable decision-makers
  - architecture-board@example.com

consulted:                     # Optional: consulted stakeholders
  - data-governance@example.com

tags:                          # Required: cross-fabric tags
  - drift
  - evidence
  - governance

affects:                       # Required: impacted UIAO documents
  - UIAO_C_01
  - UIAO_D_02

supersedes: ADR-005            # Optional
supersededBy: ADR-020          # Optional

uiao:
  id: UIAO_ADR_012             # UIAO id for this ADR document
  status: Current              # Current | Draft | Needs Replacing | Needs Creating | Deprecated
  owner: Governance Board
  tags:
    - adr
    - governance
  created: 2026-04-07
  lastUpdated: 2026-04-07
  commit: <short-hash>

summary: >
  Defines the decision to implement federated drift detection across the Drift
  and Evidence Fabrics, including responsibilities and integration contracts.

fabric: governance             # Optional primary fabric
version: 1.0                   # Optional semantic version
---

docs.title: Key Rules
docs.text:
• id must match ^ADR-[0-9]{3,}$.
  • status must be one of: Proposed, Accepted, Superseded, Deprecated, Replaced.
  • affects must contain valid UIAO_... ids.
  • uiao block follows the UIAO Document Metadata Contract.
  • Any change to this contract requires an ADR.

docs.title: Validation Rules
docs.text: Field Rule id Required, pattern ^ADR-[0-9]{3,}$ status Required, one of five allowed values date Required, ISO 8601 deciders Required, non-empty array tags Required, non-empty array affects Required, each entry matches UIAO_... pattern uiao.id Required, pattern ^UIAO_ADR_[0-9]+$ summary Required, 1-3 sentences consulted Optional supersedes Optional, pattern ^ADR-[0-9]{3,}$ supersededBy Optional, pattern ^ADR-[0-9]{3,}$

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• UIAO Document Metadata Contract
  • ADR-000 ADR Process
  • ADR Index

docs.title: UIAO Document Metadata Contract
docs.text:
This contract defines the canonical frontmatter schema for all non-ADR UIAO documents, including: Canon Overview, Appendices A-E, appendix sub-documents, governance documents, contributor guide, access page, dashboard, meta documents, and any future UIAO document.

All UIAO documents must conform to this schema. CI validation enforces required fields, allowed values, and naming patterns.

docs.title: 1. Required Frontmatter Schema
docs.text:
---
title: <Human-readable title>

uiao:
  id: UIAO_<AppendixLetter>_<Number>   # REQUIRED, canonical ID
  status: Current                      # REQUIRED: Current | Draft | Needs Replacing | Needs Creating | Deprecated
  owner: team@example.com              # REQUIRED: accountable owner
  tags:                                # REQUIRED: cross-fabric tags
    - identity
    - governance
  created: 2026-04-07                  # REQUIRED: ISO date
  lastUpdated: 2026-04-07              # REQUIRED: ISO date
  commit: <short-hash>                 # REQUIRED: provenance commit hash

summary: >                             # REQUIRED: 1-3 sentence summary
  Short description of the document purpose and scope.

version: 1.0                           # OPTIONAL: semantic version for stable documents
fabric: identity                       # OPTIONAL: primary fabric
---

docs.title: 2. Field Definitions
docs.text:
title -- Human-readable document title. Must match the H1 heading.

uiao.id -- Canonical identifier for the document. Pattern: UIAO_<AppendixLetter>_<Number>

Examples:

  • UIAO_A_01 -- Appendix A document
  • UIAO_E_03 -- Governance Plane document
  • UIAO_META_DOC_01 -- Meta contract (non-appendix)

uiao.status -- Allowed values:

Value Meaning Current Authoritative and up to date Draft Under development Needs Replacing Outdated; requires rewrite Needs Creating Placeholder exists but content missing Deprecated Superseded or retired

uiao.owner -- Team or role responsible for the document.

uiao.tags -- Cross-fabric classification. Allowed values: identity, drift, evidence, adapter, governance, meta, architecture, operations, compliance.

uiao.created / uiao.lastUpdated -- ISO dates. lastUpdated must match the commit that introduced the change.

uiao.commit -- Short git commit hash for provenance. Automatically updated by CI on merge.

summary -- 1-3 sentence description of the document purpose.

version (optional) -- Semantic version for stable documents.

fabric (optional) -- Primary fabric the document belongs to.

docs.title: 3. Example: Appendix Document
docs.text:
---
title: Adapter Lifecycle
uiao:
  id: UIAO_A_01
  status: Current
  owner: Governance Board
  tags:
    - adapter
    - governance
  created: 2026-03-01
  lastUpdated: 2026-04-07
  commit: a1b2c3d
summary: >
  Defines the canonical lifecycle for UIAO adapters, including registration,
  validation, versioning, and deprecation workflows.
fabric: adapter
version: 1.2
---

docs.title: 4. Example: Canon Overview
docs.text:
---
title: UIAO Canon Overview
uiao:
  id: UIAO_CANON_01
  status: Current
  owner: Governance Board
  tags:
    - governance
    - meta
  created: 2026-03-15
  lastUpdated: 2026-04-07
  commit: d4e5f6a
summary: >
  The authoritative overview of the Unified Identity and Access Operations (UIAO)
  architecture, its fabrics, governance model, and cross-fabric interactions.
fabric: governance
version: 1.0
---

docs.title: 5. Validation Rules (CI-Enforced)
docs.text:
Required:

  • uiao.id present and matches pattern
  • uiao.status is allowed value
  • uiao.owner present
  • uiao.tags non-empty
  • uiao.created and uiao.lastUpdated valid ISO dates
  • uiao.commit present
  • summary present
  • title matches H1

Recommended:

  • fabric present
  • version present for stable documents

Prohibited:

  • Duplicate uiao.id
  • Missing status badge in top 8 lines
  • Missing provenance footer

docs.title: 6. Governance Notes
docs.text:
• This contract is immutable except via ADR.
  • All new documents must be created using this schema.
  • CI will block merges that violate this contract.
  • Dashboard and footer rendering depend on these fields.
  • This contract is the single source of truth for UIAO document metadata.
